items.title: Iran, US Are Communicating With Violence Says Harding
items.summary: The US military attacked Iran for a second day and Tehran retaliated against American allies in the Persian Gulf, raising fears of a return to war after little progress in efforts to secure a diplomatic outcome. Why Fed Cuts Beat Rate Hikes
items.summary: Markets may be pricing yesterday's economy. iCapital's Dan Suzuki joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why slowing job growth and cooling inflation make Fed rate cuts more likely than hikes, why higher Treasury yields haven't broken the stock rally, and why AI leadership may be shifting away from chipmakers toward hyperscalers that can actually monetize their massive investments. (Source: Blo

items.title: Tech Drives US Stocks Higher as Traders Downplay Iran War Risks
items.summary: US stocks rose as investors put aside concerns over the latest flare-up between the US and Iran to pile on technology stocks after recent declines spurred dip buying.
